subject: English / First Flight
chapter_name: Poems – A Tiger in the Zoo, The Tiger, The Panther
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main events and themes of the poems
Identify simple symbols and imagery of captivity and wildness
Develop basic comprehension and summarization skills
Recognize the poet’s message about freedom, captivity, and human impact
Encourage creative expression through drawing and writing

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poems and Poets
topics_covered: Authors: Leslie Norris, Peter Niblett, Rainer Maria Rilke
Basic understanding of themes: captivity vs freedom
teaching_strategy: Introduce each poet and their style in simple language
Ask students about their experiences or feelings of confinement or freedom
student_activities: Discuss the titles and predict what each poem might be about
Share personal thoughts on being confined or free
assessment: Ask students to name the poets and poems

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – A Tiger in the Zoo
topics_covered: Explain difficult words: vivid, terrorising, concrete
Understand the tiger’s life in captivity vs the wild
teaching_strategy: Read line by line and explain simply
Encourage students to visualize the tiger in the cage and jungle
assessment: Ask students how captivity affects the tiger

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – The Tiger and The Panther
topics_covered: Discuss simple meanings of captivity, inner thoughts, and suppressed instincts
Understand the main idea of each poem
teaching_strategy: Explain line by line in simple language
Highlight tiger’s conflict between freedom and safety
student_activities: Identify words showing captivity and longing for freedom
assessment: Oral Q&A on symbols and themes

lesson_plan.title: Themes and Imagery
topics_covered: Compare the poems for similarities and differences
Discuss symbols: bars, cage, forest, eyes
Understand how poets portray tigers’ emotions
teaching_strategy: Create a simple chart comparing captivity and freedom
Use examples from poems to explain imagery
student_activities: Make a chart showing tiger in cage vs tiger in wild
Discuss how tigers’ feelings are shown through words
assessment: Oral Q&A on themes and symbols

lesson_plan.title: Creative Reflection and Expression
topics_covered: Relating poems to personal experiences or feelings
Expressing thoughts through writing or drawing
Reflecting on captivity, freedom, and nature
teaching_strategy: Encourage students to connect poems to their own experiences
Summarize key points on a chart or board
student_activities: Write a short paragraph imagining being a tiger in a cage
Draw a simple illustration of tiger in a cage or jungle
assessment: Class discussion on lessons learned from the poems

teaching_aids: Printed or projected texts of all three poems
Images of tigers in wild and in zoo cages
Flashcards with keywords and difficult words
teacher_tip: Keep explanations simple and interactive. Highlight the contrast between freedom and captivity. Encourage students to relate the poems to their own understanding of nature and emotions.
